seoTitle: How to Add Grammarly to Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Add Grammarly to Google Docs by installing the browser extension, enabling permissions, and adjusting tone settings for cleaner, more polished writing.
intro: Adding Grammarly to Google Docs gives you real-time grammar and style feedback as you write, without leaving your document. This guide covers installing the official extension, activating the widget inside your workspace, and adjusting tone settings so its suggestions match your intended audience. It's a short one-time setup that improves every document afterward.

steps.heading: Install the official extension
steps.body: Go to your browser's web store, find the verified Grammarly listing, and install it. Refresh any open Google Docs tabs afterward.

steps.heading: Activate the widget and grant permissions
steps.body: Pin the extension, log into your Grammarly account, and enable the necessary access so it can scan your active document.

steps.heading: Review inline suggestions
steps.body: As you type, correction highlights and suggestion cards appear directly in your text. Click through them to accept fixes for spelling, punctuation, and clarity.

steps.heading: Adjust tone and audience settings
steps.body: Open the extended sidebar panel to set your writing goals, such as formal, casual, or persuasive tone, so feedback fits your specific document.

steps.heading: Manage widget visibility
steps.body: Toggle the overlay on or off as needed to avoid clutter, especially when working alongside other Workspace add-ons.

faq.question: Do I need a Grammarly account to use it in Google Docs?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need to log into a Grammarly account after installing the extension for it to actively scan your documents.

faq.question: Why isn't Grammarly showing up in my document?
faq.answer: Check that the extension is enabled for the current site and that you've refreshed the tab after installation; a status badge should confirm it's active.

faq.question: Can I use Grammarly alongside Google Docs' own spelling checker?
faq.answer: Yes, both can run at the same time, and combining Grammarly's AI feedback with native tools gives a more thorough proofreading pass.

faq.question: Is adding Grammarly to Google Docs free?
faq.answer: The extension's core grammar and spelling checks are free to install and use, while advanced tone and clarity features may require a paid plan.
